introSection: RidderStrijd is a steel spinning wild mouse roller coaster built by French manufacturer Reverchon currently located at Avonturenpark Hellendoorn in Hellendoorn, Overijssel, Netherlands.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened at La Feria Chapultepec Magico in 1999 and was first located on the plot of land later occupied by Montaña Infinitum, and was relocated within the park when Montaña Infinitum was constructed in 2007. The coaster was dismantled after the park closed on 28 September 2019 and was sent to Most Revisie to be refurbished.

The ride was then sold to and was relocated to Avonturenpark Hellendoorn in 2022. Groundwork for the coaster started on 1 November 2021. It replaced the former maze that closed in 2012. In January 2022, the track pieces arrived at the park. After many delays involving the coaster, it opened on 30 March 2024.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Black track and supports. Used to have yellow track and purple supports in Mexico, featuring red supports when opening.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Raton Loco on Theme Park Review

fullText:
RidderStrijd is a steel spinning wild mouse roller coaster built by French manufacturer Reverchon currently located at Avonturenpark Hellendoorn in Hellendoorn, Overijssel, Netherlands.

The ride opened at La Feria Chapultepec Magico in 1999 and was first located on the plot of land later occupied by Montaña Infinitum, and was relocated within the park when Montaña Infinitum was constructed in 2007. The coaster was dismantled after the park closed on 28 September 2019 and was sent to Most Revisie to be refurbished.

The ride was then sold to and was relocated to Avonturenpark Hellendoorn in 2022. Groundwork for the coaster started on 1 November 2021. It replaced the former maze that closed in 2012. In January 2022, the track pieces arrived at the park. After many delays involving the coaster, it opened on 30 March 2024.



Black track and supports. Used to have yellow track and purple supports in Mexico, featuring red supports when opening.




 * Raton Loco on Theme Park Review
